current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a gated proxy comparison at `K=128` with the current best architecture and hyperparameters, changing only the optimizer from `signum` to `adam`. full
Run a `jumprelu` proxy comparison at `K=128` under the current strongest signum regime to refresh the best non-gated architecture against the stalled mainline.
Add a `lowrank_residual` prototype under `sparsify/` and run a K=128 proxy comparison under the current strongest signum regime.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The gated family has now exhausted the standard signum-side LR, aux, expansion, and low-K sweeps, while the research objective still needs a materially better K=128-quality signal. The least-bad next exploitation move is to keep the best gated K=128, EF=16, `AUXK_ALPHA=0` recipe fixed and change only the optimizer from `signum` to `adam` to test whether a different optimization dynamic can break through the current quality plateau without introducing new architectural risk.

The gated family now appears locally exhausted: the aux, LR, expansion, low-K, and optimizer sweeps have not moved the K=128 full frontier, and the recent `adam` trial regressed badly in full despite a decent proxy. The least-bad next informative move is to switch the single primary variable back to architecture and re-benchmark the strongest alternative promoted family, `jumprelu`, at K=128 under the current strongest shared training regime to see whether it can reopen a path toward better mainline quality.

The best existing promoted alternatives have now been refreshed and still sit well behind the gated frontier, so the least-bad next informative move is a genuinely new architecture family rather than another local sweep. A `lowrank_residual` SAE that uses a learned low-rank linear trunk plus a sparse residual coder could remove shared dense structure before TopK selection, which is a materially different way to attack the K=128 quality ceiling without changing the execution layer.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: The gated family now appears locally exhausted: the aux, LR, expansion, low-K, and optimizer sweeps have not moved the K=128 full frontier, and the recent `adam` trial regressed badly in full despite a decent proxy. The least-bad next informative move is to switch the single primary variable back to architecture and re-benchmark the strongest alternative promoted family, `jumprelu`, at K=128 under the current strongest shared training regime to see whether it can reopen a path toward better mainline quality.
next_steps: If `jumprelu` is competitive in proxy under the refreshed regime, keep it alive with a stabilization pass on its own family-specific settings next.
If `jumprelu` remains clearly behind gated, treat the promoted legacy families as exhausted and shift to a genuinely new architecture family afterward.
If `jumprelu` improves early-curve behavior but not final quality, follow up with a conservative family-specific tuning round rather than returning immediately to more gated sweeps.

lowrank_residual.design_hypothesis: The best existing promoted alternatives have now been refreshed and still sit well behind the gated frontier, so the least-bad next informative move is a genuinely new architecture family rather than another local sweep. A `lowrank_residual` SAE that uses a learned low-rank linear trunk plus a sparse residual coder could remove shared dense structure before TopK selection, which is a materially different way to attack the K=128 quality ceiling without changing the execution layer.
next_steps: If `lowrank_residual` runs and shows competitive proxy FVU, keep the family alive and stabilize its trunk width next.
If it runs cleanly but is clearly worse than gated and not better than prior archived families, archive it quickly and move to another genuinely new family.
If it fails at sanity or selection, treat that as an architecture-compatibility blocker and do one narrow code-fix follow-up before judging the family.

recent_performance_findings: round 41: The recent `first_step_timeout` crashes are not specific to `group_topk`: the gated AUXK=0 recovery run also advanced through the first few microbatches and then stalled at the first optimizer step. That points to a shared training-path bottleneck rather than an architecture verdict. Replacing CUDA's per-step fired-latent `cat`+advanced-index update with an incremental boolean fired mask in `sparsify/trainer.py` should remove that first-step spike and let the unresolved `group_topk` K=128 proxy run complete under the current strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 46: The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 47: The straightforward gated hyperparameter sweep has now produced only clean regressions, so the next informative move is to recover the blocked incubating family rather than spend another round on weak local tuning. `factorized_topk` still has no usable quality signal because its only attempt failed at sanity. A narrow architecture-compatibility fix inside `sparsify/` should let the same K=128 proxy prototype run and finally reveal whether extra encoder mixing can improve over plain top-k. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 49: `factorized_topk` has now failed for a concrete architecture-registration reason rather than an ambiguous quality or runtime signal: the execution layer rejects the architecture name in `SparseCoderConfig` before training begins. The next coherent move is a narrow code-fix round that wires `factorized_topk` into the existing config/class registry and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the strongest current regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=crash, termination=completed)

recent_sanity_failures.hypothesis: The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime.
